sections.paragraph:
-

(1) pr. i.q. Arab. عَكِرَ to disturb or trouble water; figuratively-

(2) to afflict any one, Jud. 11:35 often more strongly, i.q. to bring evil upon, Gen 34:30 Jos 6:18 7:25 . 1Sa 14:29, עָכַר אָבִי אֶת־הָאָרֶץ “my father troubleth the land,” 1Ki 18:17, 18 1Ki 18:18. Pro 11:17, עֹכֵר שְׁאֵרֹו אַכְזָרִי “the cruel troubleth his own flesh,” verse 29 Pro 11:29.

Niphal to be troubled, stirred up, (as grief), Psa 39:3. Part. fem. troubled, i.e. trouble, disturbance (Zerrüttung), Pro 15:6. Hence-
